message:
refactor: standardize lock-poison handling to explicit expect/unwrap

Normalizes three different lock-poison handling patterns into a
consistent style across the codebase:

- gen_server.rs (tests): Add .unwrap() to std::sync::Mutex .lock()
  calls in test code that was missing them after the parking_lot
  migration left some std Mutex instances behind
- three_lane.rs: Replace bare .lock() with .lock().expect("lock
  poisoned") on std::sync::Mutex sites that were not migrated to
  parking_lot (RuntimeState locks shared with non-async code paths)
- worker.rs: Simplify .unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) to
  .expect("lock poisoned") on Parker's std Condvar/Mutex — the
  into_inner recovery was masking real panics; failing fast on poison
  is the correct behavior for the scheduler's blocking park path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
